page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-4800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, with one thread per core. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Storage is bundled with 4800 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data shows strong performance capabilities for this hardware profile. In stress-ng testing, the server achieved a single-core CPU performance score of 2929.87 and a multi-core CPU performance score of 69533.1, both ranking in the top 25% of tested instances. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation lowers the entry barrier for a system with such high resource density, offering a massive memory footprint and substantial NVMe storage capacity without the premium associated with dedicated CPU cores.

Given its massive 192.0 GB memory capacity and 4800 GB NVMe SSD storage, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and data caching layers. The combination of strong multi-core performance and high storage capacity also makes it appropriate for enterprise web serving, content delivery nodes, and backend data processing tasks that require significant local storage and memory space.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-4800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, it delivers 192.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and a bundled 4800 GB NVMe SSD. In stress-ng benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong performance, scoring in the top 25% with 2929.87 in single-core and 69533.1 in multi-core tests. This high resource density makes it a cost-effective option for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for hosting large databases, caching systems, and enterprise web applications requiring substantial local storage and memory capacity.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-4800s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 24 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 4800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier stress-ng benchmark performance for databases.

tagline: A high-density memory and storage instance featuring twenty-four AMD EPYC cores and a massive local NVMe solid-state drive.
